aggressive.clause: The Employee's confidentiality and non-compete obligations shall be limited as follows: (1) Confidential Information shall mean only information designated in writing as confidential by the Company; (2) Non-compete duration shall be six (6) months following termination and geographic scope limited to a twenty-five (25) mile radius from the Employee's primary work location; (3) Customer restrictions shall apply only to customers with whom the Employee had documented direct contact during the final six (6) months of employment; (4) The Employee shall not be required to assign any invention conceived after termination or developed using the Employee's own resources without Company proprietary technology.
aggressive.explanation: This aggressive version provides maximum protection for the Employee by significantly reducing the non-compete duration and geographic scope, narrowing the definition of confidential information to only specifically designated materials, limiting customer restrictions to documented relationships, and preserving the Employee's rights to post-termination inventions developed independently.

balanced.clause: The Employee agrees to the following reasonable restrictions: (1) Confidential Information includes trade secrets, customer data, pricing information, and technical specifications acquired during employment; (2) Non-compete duration shall be twelve (12) months post-termination within the region where the Employee actually performed services; (3) Customer restrictions apply only to customers the Employee directly serviced during employment; (4) The Employee retains rights to prior inventions and general skills acquired during employment; (5) The Employee shall indemnify the Company only for breaches of this Agreement or gross negligence.
balanced.explanation: This balanced version protects both parties' legitimate interests by maintaining reasonable confidentiality for trade secrets, limiting the non-compete to one year with geographic restrictions, preserving the Employee's prior inventions and general skills, and providing mutual indemnification protections that cover material breaches while excluding good faith business decisions.

conservative.clause: The Employee agrees to the following modified restrictions: (1) Confidentiality obligations shall continue for thirty-six (36) months post-termination for trade secrets and twenty-four (24) months for other confidential information; (2) Non-compete duration shall be eighteen (18) months following termination with geographic scope limited to the countries where Company operates; (3) Customer restrictions shall apply to all customers of the Company during employment; (4) The Employee shall assign all inventions conceived within twelve (12) months post-termination that relate to Company's business; (5) The Employee shall indemnify the Company for all claims arising from the Employee's acts, errors, or breaches.
conservative.explanation: This conservative version preserves the majority of employer protections while acknowledging the need for judicial modification by explicitly providing that any overbroad restrictions may be modified by a court of competent jurisdiction, thereby reducing legal risk while maintaining strong intellectual property and customer relationship protections.

negotiation_email: Dear HR Director, I am pleased to accept the employment offer and propose modifying the non-compete to twelve months within my primary work region and limiting IP assignment to Company-specific technology developed during working hours. These modifications protect the Company's legitimate interests while allowing reasonable career mobility. Please advise if these terms are acceptable.
